h2h_summary: In the last 8 head-to-head matches between France and Sweden, France has won 5 times, drawn 1, and lost 2.
wc_history_home: France are two-time World Cup champions, winning on home soil in 1998 (3-0 vs Brazil) and again in Russia in 2018 (4-2 vs Croatia), and reaching the final in 2006 and 2022, making them one of the most decorated nations in tournament history.
wc_history_away: Sweden's finest World Cup moment was finishing runners-up on home soil in 1958 (losing 5-2 to Brazil), and they reached the semi-finals again in 1994 and the quarter-finals in 2018 under Janne Andersson.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: World No. 1 ranked team with elite quality across every position
Kylian Mbappe leading a devastating front three with Dembele and Olise
Defensively solid with Saliba and Upamecano providing a world-class centre-back partnership
strengths_away: Elite striker duo in Gyokeres (21 goals in 2025-26) and Isak providing multi-threat attack
Devastating on the counter-attack with Elanga providing electrifying wide pace
Graham Potter's pragmatic setup maximises squad cohesion despite limited caps
team_style_home: France deploy a structured 4-2-3-1 under Deschamps with Tchouameni and Camavinga anchoring midfield, allowing Mbappe to free-roam from a central/left position while Dembele and Olise provide directness from wide areas.
team_style_away: Sweden under Graham Potter favour compact, pragmatic defending with minimal possession, then unleash explosive direct counter-attacks exploiting the blistering pace and clinical finishing of Gyokeres, Isak, and Elanga.
recent_news: France faces Sweden in the Round of 32 with a spot in the Round of 16 on the line. Sweden suffers a major blow as Hien is injured and will miss the match.
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
